title: Set up Trigger.dev v3 for background jobs in Next.js
summary: Install Trigger.dev v3, define tasks in the /trigger directory, trigger them from API routes or Server Actions, and handle retries, delays, and concurrency.
declaration: I will set up Trigger.dev v3 for background job processing. Trigger.dev runs tasks outside your web request cycle — perfect for long-running operations, email sending, image processing, and scheduled work. If you need Inngest (event-driven) or BullMQ (Redis-backed queue) instead, say so before I begin.
